questions.explanation: EC2 stands for Elastic Compute Cloud, a scalable virtual server service.
questions.q: What does Amazon EC2 stand for?

questions.explanation: Reserved Instances provide the lowest cost for long-term commitments.
questions.q: Which EC2 pricing model offers the lowest cost for long-term usage?

questions.explanation: AMI is a template used to launch EC2 instances.
questions.q: What is an AMI in AWS EC2?

questions.explanation: EBS provides block storage attached to EC2 instances.
questions.q: Which storage is directly attached to EC2 instance?

questions.explanation: Security Groups act as virtual firewalls for EC2 instances.
questions.q: What is a Security Group in EC2?

questions.explanation: S3 stands for Simple Storage Service.
questions.q: What does S3 stand for?

questions.explanation: S3 supports objects up to 5 TB.
questions.q: What is the maximum size of an S3 object?

questions.explanation: Glacier is used for long-term archival storage.
questions.q: Which S3 storage class is cheapest for archival?

questions.explanation: VPC is a logically isolated network in AWS.
questions.q: What is a VPC?

questions.explanation: Internet Gateway allows communication between VPC and internet.
questions.q: What is an Internet Gateway in VPC?

questions.explanation: IAM manages users, roles, and permissions.
questions.q: What does IAM stand for?

questions.explanation: Roles are used to grant temporary permissions.
options: A permission set assumed by AWS services
questions.q: What is an IAM Role?

questions.explanation: AWS Lambda runs code without managing servers.
questions.q: Which AWS service provides serverless compute?

questions.explanation: Lambda maximum timeout is 15 minutes.
questions.q: Maximum execution time of AWS Lambda?

questions.explanation: CloudFormation is used to define infrastructure as code.
questions.q: What is AWS CloudFormation used for?

questions.explanation: Auto Scaling adjusts EC2 capacity automatically.
questions.q: What is EC2 Auto Scaling used for?

questions.explanation: ELB distributes incoming traffic across multiple targets.
questions.q: What is Elastic Load Balancer (ELB)?

questions.explanation: S3 Standard stores data across multiple Availability Zones.
questions.q: Which S3 feature ensures data protection across multiple AZs?

questions.explanation: NAT Gateway allows private subnet instances to access internet.
options: Outbound internet access for private subnets
questions.q: What is a NAT Gateway used for?
Which AWS service is used to launch virtual servers?
questions.explanation: EC2 is used to launch and manage virtual servers.

questions.q: What does S3 primarily provide?
questions.explanation: S3 provides scalable object storage.

questions.q: Which AWS service manages users and permissions?
questions.explanation: IAM manages identities and access control.

questions.q: What is the main purpose of a VPC?
questions.explanation: VPC provides isolated virtual networking.

questions.q: Which service runs code without servers?
questions.explanation: Lambda is serverless compute service.

questions.q: What is an AMI used for?
questions.explanation: AMI is a template for EC2 instances.

questions.q: Which storage is block-based in AWS?
questions.explanation: EBS provides block storage for EC2.

questions.q: Which service is used for monitoring AWS resources?
questions.explanation: CloudWatch monitors AWS resources.

questions.q: What is the default region-based DNS service?
questions.explanation: Route53 provides DNS services.

questions.q: Which service is used for messaging queues?
questions.explanation: SQS provides message queuing.

questions.q: What does SNS stand for?
questions.explanation: SNS is Simple Notification Service.

questions.q: Which service provides relational databases?
questions.explanation: RDS provides managed relational databases.

questions.q: Which service is used for NoSQL database?
questions.explanation: DynamoDB is a NoSQL database service.

questions.q: Which service delivers content globally?
questions.explanation: CloudFront is a CDN service.

questions.q: Which service is used for infrastructure as code?
questions.explanation: CloudFormation defines infrastructure as code.

questions.q: What is Auto Scaling used for?
questions.explanation: Auto Scaling adjusts EC2 capacity automatically.

questions.q: Which service balances traffic across EC2 instances?
questions.explanation: ELB distributes traffic across targets.

questions.q: What is EFS used for?
questions.explanation: EFS provides shared file storage.

questions.q: Which service logs API calls in AWS?
questions.explanation: CloudTrail records API activity.

questions.q: What is the use of NAT Gateway?
questions.explanation: NAT Gateway allows outbound internet for private subnets.

questions.q: Which service encrypts AWS resources?
questions.explanation: KMS manages encryption keys.

questions.q: What is VPC Peering?
questions.explanation: VPC Peering connects two VPCs privately.

questions.q: Which service is used for Docker containers?
questions.explanation: ECS runs containerized applications.

questions.q: Which service is Kubernetes in AWS?
questions.explanation: EKS is managed Kubernetes.

questions.q: What is S3 versioning used for?
questions.explanation: Versioning keeps multiple object versions.

questions.q: Which service tracks configuration changes?
questions.explanation: AWS Config tracks resource changes.

questions.q: What is CloudFormation stack?
questions.explanation: Stack is a set of AWS resources.

questions.q: Which service provides email sending?
questions.explanation: SES is Simple Email Service.

questions.q: Which database is in-memory?
questions.explanation: ElastiCache provides in-memory caching.

questions.q: What is Route53 health check used for?
questions.explanation: It monitors endpoint health.

questions.q: What is AWS KMS primarily used for?
questions.explanation: KMS manages encryption keys used to secure AWS data.

questions.q: What is the purpose of a VPC Endpoint?
options: Enable private connectivity to AWS services without internet
questions.explanation: VPC Endpoints allow private access to AWS services.

questions.q: Which service detects malicious activity in AWS accounts?
questions.explanation: GuardDuty uses machine learning to detect threats.

questions.q: What is AWS WAF used for?
questions.explanation: WAF protects web applications from attacks.

questions.q: Which service provides content delivery rules at edge locations?
questions.explanation: CloudFront delivers content using edge locations.

questions.q: What is AWS STS used for?
questions.explanation: STS provides temporary credentials for AWS access.

questions.q: Which service enables serverless workflows?
questions.explanation: Step Functions orchestrate serverless workflows.

questions.q: What is AWS EventBridge used for?
questions.explanation: EventBridge connects applications using events.

questions.q: What does AWS Shield provide?
questions.explanation: Shield protects against DDoS attacks.

questions.q: Which service manages user authentication for web apps?
questions.explanation: Cognito handles user sign-up and authentication.

questions.q: What is a Transit Gateway used for?
options: Connecting multiple VPCs and on-prem networks
questions.explanation: Transit Gateway simplifies network connectivity.

questions.q: Which AWS service provides API management?
questions.explanation: API Gateway manages APIs at scale.

questions.q: What is S3 Lifecycle policy used for?
questions.explanation: Lifecycle policies automate storage class transitions.

questions.q: Which service helps visualize AWS architecture?
questions.explanation: Architecture Center provides reference designs.

questions.q: What is AWS X-Ray used for?
questions.explanation: X-Ray helps debug distributed applications.

questions.q: What is AWS Organizations used for?
questions.explanation: AWS Organizations helps manage multiple AWS accounts under one structure.

questions.q: What does SCP in AWS Organizations stand for?
questions.explanation: SCPs define permissions boundaries for accounts in an organization.

questions.q: Which service is used for hybrid connectivity between on-premises and AWS with dedicated bandwidth?
questions.explanation: Direct Connect provides dedicated private network connectivity.

questions.q: What is Amazon ECR used for?
questions.explanation: ECR stores Docker container images.

questions.q: Which AWS service is a managed Kubernetes platform?
questions.explanation: EKS is Amazon’s managed Kubernetes service.

questions.q: What is Amazon Aurora?
questions.explanation: Aurora is a high-performance MySQL/PostgreSQL-compatible database.

questions.q: What is AWS Systems Manager used for?
options: Managing and automating EC2 and on-prem resources
questions.explanation: SSM helps manage infrastructure at scale.

questions.q: What is Amazon Redshift used for?
questions.explanation: Redshift is a data warehouse service.

questions.q: Which DynamoDB feature supports global replication?
questions.explanation: Global Tables replicate data across regions.

questions.q: What is SQS Visibility Timeout?
options: Time message is hidden after being received
questions.explanation: It prevents multiple consumers from processing the same message.

questions.q: What is SNS Fanout pattern?
questions.explanation: SNS fanout sends messages to multiple endpoints.

questions.q: What is AWS CloudHSM used for?
questions.explanation: CloudHSM provides dedicated hardware security modules.

questions.q: What is AWS Well-Architected Framework used for?
options: Designing secure and efficient cloud systems
questions.explanation: It provides best practices for architecture design.

questions.q: Which Route53 routing policy balances traffic based on latency?
questions.explanation: Latency-based routing sends users to lowest latency region.

questions.q: What is AWS VPN used for?
options: Encrypted connection between on-prem and AWS
questions.explanation: VPN provides secure tunnel over internet to AWS.

questions.q: What does EC2 stand for?
questions.explanation: EC2 means Elastic Compute Cloud.

questions.q: Which service stores objects in AWS?
questions.explanation: S3 is object storage.

questions.q: Which IAM component defines permissions?
questions.explanation: IAM policies define permissions.

questions.q: Which service is serverless compute?
questions.explanation: Lambda runs code without servers.

questions.q: What is a VPC used for?
questions.explanation: VPC provides isolated networking.
CloudWatch monitors AWS resources and applications.
questions.q: What is AWS CloudWatch used for?
